data.title: WHT HSE PRESS SECTY LEAVITT Q&A: PRES TRUMP DOESN'T LIKE TO SEE EPSTEIN CASE COVERED LIKE THE BIGGEST STORY; TRUMP ADMIN BEING FULLY TRANSPARENT
Market Chatter: US Set to Impose 93.5% Antidumping Duty on Graphite From China
data.description:
The US Commerce Department levied preliminary antidumping duties of 93.5% on graphite from China, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

A final ruling may be announced on Dec. 5, the Commerce Department said in a preliminary determination, according to the Bloomberg report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Liberty Defense Down After Upsizing Equity Financing to $4.4 Million
data.description:
Liberty Defense on Thursday said it upsized a non-brokered private-placement financing to $4.4 million from $3.75 million.

The company is now offering 20-million units priced at $0.22 each and made up of a share and a one-year warrant to buy a share for $0.35.

Proceeds will be used to buy inventory to further the production of HEXWAVE technology units, investor relations and marketing initiatives, operating expenses and for general working capital purposes. The offering is scheduled to close on or about July 22.

Shares of the company were last seen down $0.005 at $0.23 on the TSX Venture Exchange.

data.title: Soy futures exceed one-week high, corn futures retreat from gains
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures hit their highest level in more than a week on Thursday as technical buying helped the market recover from a three-month low reached on Monday, analysts said.

Corn futures retreated after rising for the previous three sessions in a rebound from contract lows set on Monday.

Both markets continued to face pressure from expectations for large U.S. autumn harvests and mostly favorable crop weather in the world's biggest corn-producing country and second-largest soybean producer.

"We're marching to a crop, and the weather looks non-threatening," said Don Roose, president of brokerage U.S. Commodities.

CBOT soybeans were up 6 cents at $10.26-1/2 a bushel by 12:30 p.m. CDT (1730 GMT), after rising on Wednesday on hopes for increased demand for U.S. supplies from Indonesia and top importer China.

U.S. soybean export sales in the week ended July 10 reached 529,600 tons for 2025-26 shipment, above analysts' expectations for 150,000 to 400,000 tons.

For corn, new-crop U.S. export sales of 565,900 tons were within analysts' estimates.

CBOT corn was down 3-3/4 cents at $4.20-1/4 a bushel. The December contract (CZ25), which represents the crop that farmers will harvest this autumn, set a low of $4.07-1/2 on Monday.

"While price weakness attracted opportunistic demand, a favourable weather outlook could well see lows tested again in the weeks ahead," Rabobank analysts said.

U.S. President Donald Trump added pressure on corn prices, analysts said, by saying on Wednesday that Coca-Cola had agreed to use cane sugar in its U.S. beverages. Coca-Cola produced for the U.S. market is typically sweetened with corn syrup.

Analysts estimated that a little more than 400 million bushels of corn are used annually to make high fructose corn syrup, though not all of that is put into beverages.

U.S. corn production is projected at 15.7 billion bushels for 2025-26, and total usage is expected to be about 15.4 billion bushels, according to USDA data.

In CBOT wheat , most-active futures were down 8 cents at $5.33-1/4 a bushel.

data.title: WHT HSE PRESS SECTY LEAVITT Q&A: REPEATS, IF NO RUSSIA-UKRAINE CEASEFIRE IN 50 DAYS THEN SECONDARY TARIFFS TO BE APPLIED TO CUSTOMERS OF RUSSIA OIL
Sector Update: Financial Stocks Advance in Afternoon Trading
data.description:
Financial stocks were rising in Thursday afternoon trading, with the NYSE Financial Index up 0.4% and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) adding 0.5%.

The Philadelphia Housing Index was increasing 0.2%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE) was shedding 0.5%.

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) was down 0.3% at $119,074, and the yield for 10-year US Treasuries was slightly higher at 4.46%.

In economic news, retail sales rose 0.6% in June, outpacing the 0.1% forecast and rebounding from a 0.9% drop in May. Excluding motor vehicles, sales climbed 0.5%; excluding both motor vehicles and flat gasoline station figures, sales increased 0.6%.

The NAHB housing market index ticked up to 33 in July from 32 in June, matching estimates. Still, the gauge remains below 41 recorded a year ago.

The Philadelphia Fed's manufacturing index surged to 15.9 in July from minus 4, ahead of forecasts for a minus 1 print.

In corporate news, Travelers shares gained 3% after it reported higher Q2 core income and revenue.

A consortium led by BlackRock is nearing a deal to invest $10 billion in the infrastructure of Saudi Aramco's Jafurah gas project in Saudi Arabia, Reuters reported. BlackRock shares gained 2.1%.

US Bancorp reported higher-than-expected Q2 earnings on Thursday as revenue rose year on year, while the lender now sees full-year revenue growth at the low end of its guidance. Its shares were falling 1.9%.

The EU has issued a legal warning to the Spanish government for hindering BBVA's planned acquisition of rival Banco Sabadell, Bloomberg reported. BBVA shares were down 0.8%.

data.title: Taiwan Semi Tops Estimates with Q2 Profit Boom
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM), known for making chips for Apple , Nvidia and AMD , reported net income of NT$398.3 billion, about $13.5 billion, in the second quarter. That's a 60.7% jump even as revenue climbed 44.4% to $30.07 billion.

The surge was powered by high?performance computing orders, which accounted for 60 percent of sales, and a ramp in three?nanometer production now making up 24 percent of wafer revenue. Five?nanometer stayed at about 36 percent, while seven?nanometer dipped to 14%.

A gross margin of 58.6% cushioned the bottom line and beat expectations. CFO Wendell Huang said robust AI and HPC demand will carry into the third quarter, where TSMC sees revenue around $32.4 billion at the midpoint.

TSMC plans to spend up to $42 billion on capacity upgrades this year. With AI workloads still accelerating, execution on its next?gen nodes and cost control will determine whether it can keep its edge.

data.title: MP Materials extends surge after boosted $650 mln stock sale
data.description:
** Shares of MP Materials rising in afternoon trading and last up 4.7% at $61.32 after upsized equity raise

** MP shares have more than doubled over the past six sessions and are on track for fresh record high close

** Las Vegas, Nevada-based rare earths miner early Thurs said it sold ~11.8 mln shares at $55 for $650 mln gross proceeds to fund expansion of operations and growth

** Offering size increased from $ 500 mln upon launch late Weds. JP Morgan and Goldman Sachs acting as joint lead bookrunners, joined by Morgan Stanley

** Last Thurs, MP sealed multibillion-dollar deal with the U.S. government to boost rare earth magnets output to break China's grip

** On Tues, Apple said to invest $500 mln as iPhone maker seeks to localize its mineral supply chain

** MP has ~163.5 mln shares outstanding for $10 bln current market cap

** Shares ended 2024 at $15.60

data.title: Stonepeak to Invest USD 1.3 Billion in Princeton Digital Group
data.description:
With USD 2.5 billion raised this year, PDG positions itself to accelerate scale by organic growth and M&A

SINGAPORE, July 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Princeton Digital Group (PDG), Asia Pacific's leading data center operator, today announced the signing of a definitive agreement with Stonepeak, a leading alternative investment firm specializing in infrastructure and real assets. The firm will make a preferred equity investment of USD 1.3 billion in PDG to support its continued expansion across Asia Pacific.

This investment follows PDG's recently announced USD 1.2 billion debt financing. Together, Stonepeak's investment and the recent debt financing bring the total capital raised by PDG in 2025 to USD 2.5 billion across equity and debt, reinforcing the company's position as a leading provider of hyperscale infrastructure in Asia.

With a current portfolio of over 1.1 gigawatts across six countries, PDG is one of the region's largest and fastest-growing data center platforms. With marquee global investors--Warburg Pincus, Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, Mubadala, and now Stonepeak--PDG is uniquely positioned with long-term backing from some of the world's most respected capital partners.

Stonepeak's long-term capital will support the company's next phase of growth, including both greenfield development and M&A, across both established and emerging Asia Pacific markets. Warburg Pincus will continue to be PDG's largest shareholder.

"This milestone investment from Stonepeak is a strong endorsement of PDG's strategy, execution, and sustained value creation," said Rangu Salgame, Chairman, CEO and Co-founder of PDG. "Stonepeak shares our deep conviction in the unprecedented growth of AI and cloud across Asia Pacific. With this partnership, PDG is uniquely positioned to scale with speed, continue being the trusted provider to the world's most demanding hyperscalers, and further consolidate its position as a market leader in the region."

"PDG has established itself as one of the clear leaders among digital infrastructure platforms in the Asia Pacific region. The company's track record of execution, top-tier management team, and significant power bank in critical hub markets in APAC positions it well to serve the continued demand from hyperscalers and AI-driven platforms in the region, " said Andrew Thomas, Senior Managing Director at Stonepeak. "This investment is a quality fit for our Asia infrastructure strategy, and we look forward to partnering with PDG's management team, Warburg Pincus and existing shareholders to propel the company's next phase of growth."

Ellen Ng, Co-Head of Asia Real Estate at Warburg Pincus, said, "As a founding investor of PDG we've always believed in the founders' vision and exceptional execution capabilities and supported the company's evolution into Asia's preeminent data center platform. This latest investment by Stonepeak is a strong validation of PDG's market leadership and long-term strategy. As PDG's largest shareholder, we are excited to welcome a like-minded partner to help propel the company into its next phase of growth, supporting the surging demand for AI and cloud infrastructure across the region."

PDG is being advised by Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan, and Latham & Watkins as legal counsel. Barclays is serving as financial advisor and Sidley Austin LLP is serving as legal counsel to Stonepeak.

About Princeton Digital Group

Princeton Digital Group (PDG) is a leading developer and operator of Internet infrastructure. Headquartered in Singapore with presence and operations in Singapore, Japan, India, Indonesia, China, and Malaysia, its portfolio of data centers powers the expansion of hyperscalers and enterprises in the fastest-growing digital economies across Asia. For more information, visit www.princetondg.com or follow us on LinkedIn.

About Stonepeak

Stonepeak is a leading alternative investment firm specializing in infrastructure and real assets with approximately $73 billion of assets under management. Through its investment in defensive, hard-asset businesses globally, Stonepeak aims to create value for its investors and portfolio companies, with a focus on downside protection and strong risk-adjusted returns. Stonepeak, as sponsor of private equity and credit investment vehicles, provides capital, operational support, and committed partnership to grow investments in its target sectors, which include transport and logistics, digital infrastructure, energy and energy transition, and real estate. Stonepeak is headquartered in New York with offices in Houston, Washington, D.C., London, Hong Kong, Seoul, Singapore, Sydney, Tokyo, Abu Dhabi, and Riyadh. For more information, please visit www.stonepeak.com.

For more information please contact:

Princeton Digital Group

Selena Sheikh

Selena.sheikh@princetondg.com

Finn Partners for Princeton Digital Group

PDG.ASIA@finnpartners.com

Kate Beers / Maya Brounstein for Stonepeak

corporatecomms@stonepeak.com

+1 (646) 540-5225

SOURCE Princeton Digital Group

data.title: L3Harris unveils new long-range missile with eye on China in the Pacific
data.description:
L3Harris Technologies has unveiled two new missiles that aim to provide less expensive long-range strike options for the U.S. military as it restocks its supplies while looking for arms to deter China's ambitions in the Pacific.

The "Red Wolf" and "Green Wolf" missile launch comes as the concept of "affordable mass" has gained prominence due to the conflicts in Ukraine and Israel, which have underscored the need for plentiful, cost-effective weapons. This strategy focuses on having a large number of relatively inexpensive munitions ready for deployment, ensuring military readiness and adaptability.

These multi-role missiles with a range of more than 200 nautical miles can hit moving targets like ships, such as in the Pacific Ocean where range is important. Lockheed Martin and RTX currently dominate the space in the U.S. market.

The most basic version of L3's new missile would cost in the $300,000-range once production has reached full rate, L3 executives told Reuters in an interview.

Currently, the low-rate initial production run is being generated in Ashburn, Virginia, with the customers like the Pentagon eyeing buying about 1,000 per year, L3 executives said.

The Red Wolf is a traditional missile focused on long-range precision strikes, while the Green Wolf specializes in electronic warfare, equipped with capabilities for electronic attack and intelligence gathering.

L3Harris has conducted over 40 successful test flights, demonstrating the reliability and effectiveness of these systems.

data.title: Update: Gold Trading Lower as Dollar Climbs After U.S. Retail Sales Rose Above Expectations Last Month
data.description:
(Updates prices.)

Gold traded lower midafternoon on Thursday as the dollar moved up after U.S. retail sales rose above expectations last month.

Gold for August delivery was last seen down US$16.30 to US$3.342.80 per ounce.

The U.S. Census Bureau on Thursday reported retail sales rose by 0.6% in June from the prior month, up from a drop of 0.9% in May, and well ahead of the consensus estimate for a rise of 0.2% according to Marketwatch. Weekly initial jobless claims of 221,000 were also under expectations, down from 228,000 last week and under the consensus estimate of 234,000 new claims.

The dollar moved up following the reports, bearish for commodities priced in the currency. The ICE dollar index was last seen up 0.35 points to 98.74, the highest since June 20.

Treasury yields were mixed, with the U.S. two-year note last seen up 2.1 basis points to 3.919%, while the 10-year note was paying 4.462%, down 0.01 points.

data.title: Google Just Signed the Biggest Hydropower Deal in Corporate History
data.description:
Google (GOOGL, Financials) is locking in a massive amount of clean power and it's doing it with water. The company just announced a $3 billion agreement with Brookfield Asset Management (BAM.TO) to secure up to 3 gigawatts of U.S. hydropower, making this the largest-ever corporate deal of its kind for hydroelectric energy.

The power will come from two upgraded hydropower facilities in Pennsylvania, and the contracts will stretch out over 20 years. It's a big move aimed at feeding Google's growing appetite for clean electricity as it builds out AI-focused data centers across the Mid-Atlantic.

That's not all according to Semafor, Google is also putting down $25 billion to expand its data infrastructure in Pennsylvania and nearby states over the next two years.

"This collaboration with Brookfield is a significant step forward, ensuring clean energy supply in the PJM region where we operate," said Amanda Peterson Corio, who leads energy strategy for Google's data centers.

The deal is part of a much bigger play. The company has already signed first-of-their-kind clean energy contracts including geothermal and advanced nuclear and is now working with PJM Interconnection, the U.S.'s largest grid operator, to speed up how quickly new power sources can come online using AI.

Alphabet's president, Ruth Porat, is expected to talk more about the announcement this week at an AI summit in Pittsburgh. U.S. President Donald Trump will also attend, with total AI and energy-related investments at the event expected to hit $70 billion.

Google said it's planning to expand this hydropower model to other states across the Mid-Atlantic and Midwest, making it a foundational part of how the company powers its AI future.

data.title: US Set to Impose 93.5% Anti-Dumping Duty on Chinese Graphite, Bloomberg Reports
Lucid Soars 40% After Uber Unveils Global Robotaxi Plan with Nuro
data.description:
July 17 - Lucid Group (LCID) shares surged more than 40% in Thursday's morning session after the company secured a major partnership with Uber to develop a high-end robotaxi fleet.

Under the agreement, Uber will inject $300 million into Lucid and commit an additional multi-hundred-million dollar investment into autonomous driving startup Nuro. The trio will collaborate on a global robotaxi program built around Nuro's self-driving technology.

Uber plans to deploy at least 20,000 Lucid vehicles equipped with the Nuro Driver system over the next six years. These robotaxis will be operated either directly by Uber or through its affiliated fleet partners, and will only be accessible via the Uber platform.

The project marks an aggressive move to challenge established players in autonomous mobility like Tesla and Waymo, a subsidiary of Alphabet's Google .

Autonomous vehicles have enormous potential to transform our cities, said Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi in the release. He noted the collaboration is intended to make autonomous driving safer and more accessible to users worldwide.

The partnership positions Lucid and Uber as serious contenders in the evolving robotaxi race, with initial deployment expected to begin in key global markets

data.title: WHT HSE PRESS SECTY LEAVITT Q&A: 'GENIUS ACT' ON STABLECOIN TO MAKE USA CRYPTO CAPITAL OF THE WORLD; PASSING HOUSE AND TO BE SIGNED INTO LAW SOON
U.S. Bancorp Lending Profit Margin Dented by Consumer Loan Sale — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. Bancorp's sale of $5.5 billion in consumer loans in 2Q weighed on lending profit margins and average earning assets, Edward Jones analyst James Shanahan says in a research note. An increase in residential mortgage paydowns also weighed on those assets and margins, the analyst says. Management is guiding for a modest recovery in lending income to start in 3Q, Shanahan says. "We remain focused on action and initiatives to strengthen net interest income, and those efforts are fully reflected in our guidance," CFO John Stern says on a call with analysts. Shares slide 2% to $44.76. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1354 ET - U.S. Bancorp's sale of $5.5 billion in consumer loans in 2Q weighed on lending profit margins and average earning assets, Edward Jones analyst James Shanahan says in a research note. An increase in residential mortgage paydowns also weighed on those assets and margins, the analyst says. Management is guiding for a modest recovery in lending income to start in 3Q, Shanahan says. "We remain focused on action and initiatives to strengthen net interest income, and those efforts are fully reflected in our guidance," CFO John Stern says on a call with analysts. Shares slide 2% to $44.76. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1349 ET - President Trump's Truth Social post stating that Coca-Cola has agreed to remove high fructose corn syrup from their soda, in favor of using sugar sourced from sugarcane. But high fructose corn syrup is a big consumer of the corn produced in the U.S. — and the majority of sugar made in the U.S. comes from sugar beets, not sugarcane. The USDA says that sugar beets accounted for 55% to 60% of total sugar production since the early 2000s. But most U.S. farmers don't grow beets for sugar, never mind sugarcane — with sugar crops accounting for less than 1% of cash receipts for U.S. farmers on average. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1336 ET - Markets for biodiversity credits are more local compared to carbon offsets because buyers usually seek to compensate for their use of local resources such as timberlands, says Aleksandra Holmlund, CEO of Qarlbo Biodiversity, a Swedish forestry investment and management company. "Carbon credits are sort of a global product because the carbon dioxide molecule is the same everywhere in the world," she says. "That's not the case with biodiversity." Qarlbo earlier this year sold biodiversity credits from its forest holdings in Louisiana in what industry consultants deemed the first such deal in the U.S. Regulators could bolster the fledgling market by issuing recommendations that address things such as how credits should be created and sold, Holmlund says. "That's not to say we need laws and regulations but just recommendations." (luis.garcia@wsj.com; @lhvgarcia)

1320 ET - The tone of Elevance Health's analyst call was even more cautious than expected, especially when it came to Medicaid, according to Mizuho in a note. The analysts say that although Medicaid managed care plans are required to receive actuarial sound rates, it could take two or three rate cycles for rates to fully capture current trends. This, plus uncertainty around the expiration of enhanced advanced premium tax credits and incremental Medicaid dis-enrollment, makes the company's earnings trajectory more unclear, the analysts say. Now, full margin recovery looks like it won't be until 2027 or 2028, they say. Elevance slides 11%. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1238 ET - Business trips are back in business. United Airlines Chief Executive Scott Kirby says in an interview with CNBC that air travel among businesses has come back after a sluggish period around April, when many companies were holding off on expenses to see how tariffs would pan out. Business demand has accelerated by a double-digit percentage over the past three weeks, Kirby says in a call with analysts. "You see more and more businesses coming off the sidelines, getting unfrozen and I think getting back to business," he tells CNBC. United shares are up 1.2%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1235 ET - United Airlines sees an opportunity to take more market share from low-cost carriers, who are suffering from cost convergence, Chief Executive Scott Kirby says on a call. Particularly at high-cost airports, low-cost carriers are dealing with rising expenses, while airlines like United and Delta have been able to operate more efficiently. "I look at every airline that's not named United or Delta. I can find at every single one of them a double-digit percentage of their route network that loses money," Kirby says. The only way for those carriers to improve margins would be to stop operating at money-losing routes, which opens market share for United. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1233 ET - Elevance Health says a slower-than-expected deceleration in rising Medicaid costs will hurt its bottom line this year, guiding for adjusted earnings per-share of about $30, compared to a midpoint of $34.50 in its previous range. "This reset reflects the same pressures that others in the sector have now confirmed," the company says during a call with analysts. Other health insurers, like UnitedHealth Group and Molina, have recently warned that high Medicare and Medicaid costs could pressure profits this year. Shares fall 11%.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1231 ET - While most investors likely assumed a low probability of Couche-Tard securing a deal to buy 7-Eleven parent Seven & I, the decision to end the pursuit after nearly a year offers the definitive conclusion investors were probably looking for, BMO Capital Markets' Tamy Chen suggests. The analysts notes Couche-Tard's shares had been range-bound in recent weeks. Share buybacks should resume, and attention can now focus on the company's existing business which is still experiencing some pressures, Chen says. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

1209 ET - Wal-Mart de Mexico's 10% drop in 2Q net profit and unexpected slip in Ebitda reflect higher-than-anticipated expenses, while the 8.3% increase in sales was in line with estimates, analysts note. "Just like other less consolidated players in the market, Walmex faces significant challenges with the rise in operating costs," says Marcela Muñoz of Vector Casa de Bolsa. Walmex shares are down 5.8% on the Mexican stock exchange, while the benchmark IPC index is up 0.5%. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1205 ET - United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby sees the U.S. economy stabilizing, which he believes is driving an uptick in air-travel demand over the past three weeks. Kirby says on a call with analysts that the passage of the budget reconciliation bill, along with easing tensions in the Middle East, are behind the six-point increase in demand United clocked in recent weeks. Even tariffs, though not yet set in stone, are creating fewer jitters as "the market and most businesses have a much better read on how they'll manage in a narrower range of outcomes," Kirby says. United shares rise 2.6%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1200 ET - Starbucks' stock may be running too hot. Credit card, foot traffic and app data all indicate the company will underperform Wall Street's third and fourth-quarter comparative sales consensus, says Jefferies analysts, who cut the stock to underperform. Meanwhile, the value of Starbucks' possible sale of its China business is likely lower than has been rumored and large investments in people and technology could weigh down near-term earnings, the analysts say. The company also has questionable strategic priorities, analysts say, including a focus on hot drinks and in-store experience over cold beverages and drive-thrus. Jefferies maintains its price target at $76, well below its current price of $92.55. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1107 ET - PepsiCo will continue to lean into protein. "Protein is clearly a subsegment in our food and beverage categories that is growing fast," executives say on a call with analysts. "Consumers are adopting protein solutions in their diets at a pace that wasn't the case a few months back, or a few years back." The snacks and soda maker is adding protein to brands including PopCorners chips and Quaker snacks. The company plans to eventually add protein to its larger brands, planning several rollouts for Q4 and Q1 that investors will "hear more about in due time." Shares rise 6.4%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Metropolitan Bank Declares Initial Dividend; Approves $50 Million Share Buyback Plan
data.description:
Metropolitan Bank said Thursday its board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per share, its first since the initial public offering in 2017. It is payable Aug. 11 to holders of record on July 28.

The company's board also authorized a new share repurchase program to buy up to $50 million shares, the company said.

The company's shares, which were halted earlier, are up 1.7% in recent trading.

data.title: Metropolitan Bank Holding Starts Dividend, Authorizes Buyback
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Metropolitan Bank Holding declared its first-ever dividend as a public company and its board authorized a buyback of shares.

The new dividend would be a quarterly payout of 15 cents, the company said. In April, the New York company said it planned to begin paying a quarterly dividend.

Metropolitan Bank Holding also said it would repurchase up to $50 million of common stock.

Shares of the company were halted due to news pending, and were up 0.8%, to $74.30.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Sector Update: Consumer Stocks Rise in Afternoon Trading
data.description:
Consumer stocks rose Thursday afternoon with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) gaining 0.5% and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) adding 0.3%.

In corporate news, PepsiCo shares jumped 7% after fiscal Q2 results showed a surprise revenue increase and an improved full-year earnings outlook amid easing foreign-exchange headwinds.

Uber plans to deploy at least 20,000 Lucid vehicles that will be equipped with Nuro's autonomous vehicle technology. Lucid shares surged 39%, and Uber was little changed.

United Airlines shares rose 2.3%, a day after the company reported Q2 adjusted earnings that fell less than expected by analysts.

data.title: Tesla faces lawsuit over insurance claims; shares dip
data.description:
** Tesla faces consumer lawsuit in California accusing its insurance unit of delaying and minimizing payouts; shares last down 1.3%

** Lawsuit filed in Los Angeles Superior Court alleges TSLA Insurance failed to maintain sufficient claims-handling procedures, potentially affecting thousands of motorists

** Reuters investigation in 2023 highlighted consumer complaints and regulatory scrutiny over TSLA's insurance claims processing

** TSLA stock has plunged about 21% YTD, underperforming Nasdaq , which is up 8%

** Stock is about 35% below its intraday record high of $488.54 on Dec. 18, 2024

** TSLA recently traded at 137 times expected earnings vs its avg forward PE of 90 over past five years, per LSEG

data.title: Majority of U.S. Sugar Production Comes From Beets — Market Talk
data.description: President Trump's Truth Social post stating that Coca-Cola has agreed to remove high fructose corn syrup from their soda, in favor of using sugar sourced from sugarcane. But high fructose corn syrup is a big consumer of the corn produced in the U.S. — and the majority of sugar made in the U.S. comes from sugar beets, not sugarcane. The USDA says that sugar beets accounted for 55% to 60% of total sugar production since the early 2000s. But most U.S. farmers don't grow beets for sugar, never mind sugarcane — with sugar crops accounting for less than 1% of cash receipts for U.S. farmers on average. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care Stocks Fall Thursday Afternoon
data.description:
Health care stocks declined Thursday afternoon, with the NYSE Health Care Index down 1.5% and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) falling 1.3%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) rose 0.2%.

In corporate news, Elevance Health shares tumbled past 11% after the company cut its 2025 earnings guidance.

Abbott Laboratories issued a Q3 earnings outlook below market estimates despite recording better-than-expected Q2 results, while the healthcare company tightened its full-year profit guidance. Its shares dropped 8%.

Novartis posted Q2 earnings above market estimates, but revenue fell short of expectations, while the company reiterated its full-year sales growth outlook. Its shares were down 3.5%.
